assertion_results.text: Tasks target both 'trustify-backend' and 'trustify-ui' repositories with at least one task per repo
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks: task-1 (trustify-backend), task-2 (trustify-backend), task-3 (trustify-backend), task-4 (trustify-backend), task-5 (trustify-backend), task-11 (trustify-backend). Frontend tasks: task-6 (trustify-ui), task-7 (trustify-ui), task-8 (trustify-ui), task-9 (trustify-ui), task-10 (trustify-ui). Both repos have multiple tasks.

assertion_results.text: Backend tasks have lower task numbers than the frontend tasks that depend on them
assertion_results.evidence: Backend implementation tasks are 2-5, frontend implementation tasks are 6-10. The dependency graph in impact-map.md shows backend tasks (2-5) run first, then frontend tasks (6-10). No frontend task has a lower number than a backend task it depends on. The feature-branch workflow with bookend tasks (1=create-branch, 11=merge-branch) uses implicit ordering via task numbers: all backend tasks (2-5) precede all frontend tasks (6-10).

assertion_results.text: UI-facing frontend tasks (pages, components) reference Figma design context mentioning specific PatternFly components and visual specifications — API-layer frontend tasks (API types, client functions, hooks) are exempt from this requirement
assertion_results.evidence: UI-facing tasks: task-8 (SbomComparePage) references Figma extensively (21 mentions of Figma/PatternFly) including specific components: PatternFly Select (typeahead), Button, Dropdown, ExpandableSection, Badge, Table (composable), EmptyState, Skeleton, CodeBranchIcon, SeverityBadge. task-9 (SbomListPage selection) references PatternFly Table select variant, PatternFly Button, PatternFly Badge, and Figma design context (7 mentions). API-layer tasks exempt: task-6 (API types/client) has 0 Figma/PatternFly mentions, task-7 (comparison hook) has 0 Figma/PatternFly mentions — both are API-layer tasks and correctly exempt. task-10 (E2E/MSW mocks) has 0 mentions but is a testing task, not a UI-facing component task.

assertion_results.text: Cross-repo dependency ordering is enforced: either at least one frontend task explicitly depends on a backend task in its Dependencies section, or all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan uses feature-branch workflow with TC-9003 as the shared feature branch. All intermediate tasks (2-10) target TC-9003 branch. Backend tasks (2-5) have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (6-10). The dependency graph in impact-map.md shows Task 1 (create-branch) as the root, backend tasks 2-5 on one chain, frontend tasks 6-10 on another, all converging on Task 11 (merge-branch). This satisfies the second condition: 'all tasks target a shared feature branch where backend tasks have lower task numbers than frontend tasks (implicit ordering via feature-branch workflow)'.

assertion_results.text: Frontend tasks reference paths from repo-frontend.md; backend tasks reference paths from repo-backend.md
assertion_results.evidence: Backend tasks reference paths under modules/fundamental/src/sbom/ (model/, service/, endpoints/) and tests/api/ — all present in repo-backend.md directory tree. Specific examples: task-2 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (in manifest), task-4 modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (in manifest). Frontend tasks reference paths under src/api/, src/hooks/, src/pages/, src/components/, src/routes.tsx, src/App.tsx, tests/mocks/, tests/e2e/ — all present in repo-frontend.md directory tree. Specific examples: task-6 modifies src/api/models.ts and src/api/rest.ts (both in manifest), task-9 modifies src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in manifest).

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 tasks have a '## Repository' section with exactly one repository name. Tasks 1-5, 11: 'trustify-backend'. Tasks 6-10: 'trustify-ui'. No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 task files contain all four required sections: '## Target Branch' (count=1 in all), '## Description' (count=1 in all), '## Acceptance Criteria' (count=1 in all), '## Test Requirements' (count=1 in all). Verified via grep -c for each section header across all task files.

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the corresponding mock repository structure manifests (repo-backend.md or repo-frontend.md), not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: Backend task file paths: task-2 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/comparison.rs (new file in existing model/ dir from manifest), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/mod.rs (in manifest). task-3 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/compare.rs (new file in existing service/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/mod.rs (in manifest). task-4 creates modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/compare.rs (new file in existing endpoints/ dir), modifies modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/mod.rs (in manifest). task-5 creates tests/api/sbom_compare.rs (new file in existing tests/api/ dir). Frontend task file paths: task-6 modifies src/api/models.ts and src/api/rest.ts (both in manifest). task-7 creates src/hooks/useSbomComparison.ts (new file in existing hooks/ dir). task-8 creates files under src/pages/SbomComparePage/ (following page structure pattern from manifest), modifies src/routes.tsx and src/App.tsx (both in manifest). task-9 modifies src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx (in manifest). task-10 creates tests/mocks/fixtures/sbom-comparison.json and tests/e2e/sbom-compare.spec.ts (new files in existing dirs), modifies tests/mocks/handlers.ts (in manifest). All paths follow existing directory structures from the manifests. Note: task-5 references 'tests/api/mod.rs' in Files to Modify but hedges with 'or test runner configuration' — this file is not in the manifest but the caveat makes it acceptable.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every non-bookend task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance — bookend tasks (create-branch, merge-branch) are exempt as they omit Implementation Notes by design
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks (1, 11) have no Implementation Notes section — correctly exempt. Non-bookend tasks: task-2 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/details.rs, summary.rs, modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs with specific struct names (SbomDetails, SbomSummary, AdvisorySummary). task-3 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (SbomService), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (PackageService), modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs (AdvisoryService), common/src/error.rs (AppError). task-4 references modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/get.rs and list.rs with specific handler patterns and Axum extractors. task-5 references tests/api/sbom.rs and tests/api/advisory.rs with specific test patterns. task-6 references src/api/models.ts, src/api/rest.ts, src/api/client.ts with specific function names (fetchSboms). task-7 references src/hooks/useSboms.ts and src/hooks/useSbomById.ts with specific hook patterns. task-8 references src/components/SeverityBadge.tsx, EmptyStateCard.tsx, FilterToolbar.tsx, src/hooks/useSboms.ts, src/pages/SbomDetailPage/components/PackageTable.tsx, AdvisoryList.tsx. task-9 references src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.tsx, src/components/FilterToolbar.tsx, src/routes.tsx. task-10 references tests/mocks/handlers.ts, tests/mocks/fixtures/sboms.json, tests/e2e/sbom-list.spec.ts, tests/setup.ts, src/pages/SbomListPage/SbomListPage.test.tsx. All reference specific file paths and code patterns.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section — either all set to 'main' (direct-to-main workflow) or bookend tasks set to 'main' with intermediate tasks set to the feature issue ID (feature-branch workflow)
assertion_results.evidence: Feature-branch workflow is used. Bookend tasks: task-1 (create-branch) has Target Branch = main, task-11 (merge-branch) has Target Branch = main. All intermediate tasks (2-10) have Target Branch = TC-9003 (the feature issue ID). This matches the feature-branch workflow pattern exactly.

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 task files contain a digest line matching the format '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. Each hash is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Specific examples: task-1: sha256-md:c5db740a346d48d99ad5373075b878bca456c0990854d3a992200eb5f96118f4, task-2: sha256-md:d1a2e346dad86857aeffeee573a2266fc1875b54a873c023575f6df15d672dd8, task-11: sha256-md:157f39d7d7ebdfbf174ad7077a6ad6fdc2ddb6cc71bd000d90a2666f4c9b3c9a. All use the 'sha256-md:' prefix. All hashes are unique (not placeholder/example values). Note: cannot verify the digests were computed by re-fetching from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py from file output alone, but the format is correct.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: No task file contains any convention-applicability enrichment. grep for 'CONVENTIONS', 'Per CONVENTIONS', and 'Applies:' across all task files found zero matches for convention-applicability rationale lines. The word 'conventions' appears only in generic references to coding patterns (e.g., 'serialization and documentation conventions' in task-2, 'test conventions' in task-5), not in the prescribed 'Per CONVENTIONS.md §...' format with 'Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>' rationale. Convention-aware enrichment was not performed at all.
